You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the zephyr codebase.
Zephyr is a scalable real-time operating system (RTOS) for connected, resource-constrained devices. It includes device drivers, HAL, networking (BLE, Wi-Fi, Thread), USB, shell, logging, and supports multiple architectures (ARM, RISC-V, x86, Xtensa). Code is primarily C.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
Think step by step. First understand the code, then investigate, then fix.

There is a race condition on shared data accessed from multiple threads without synchronization downstream of function unicast_client_stop_cb in the tests/bluetooth/tester subsystem.
The function is located at tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c:934.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

I'll investigate the race condition in the `unicast_client_stop_cb` function and fix the synchronization issue. Let me first examine the code to understand the problem.

Now I can see the issue. The `unicast_client_stop_cb` function accesses `u_stream->ase_id` without proper synchronization. The `u_stream` points to a `btp_bap_unicast_stream` structure which is part of the global `connections` array. Multiple threads could potentially access this data concurrently.

Now I understand the issue. The `unicast_client_stop_cb` function accesses shared data (`u_stream->ase_id`) without proper synchronization. The global `connections` array can be accessed by multiple threads concurrently, and there's no mutex protection around accessing the stream data.

Let me check if there are any existing mutexes for protecting the connections array:

<bash>grep -n "mutex\|K_MUTEX" /repo/tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c</bash>
Exit code: 1

There are no existing mutexes for protecting the connections array. I need to add synchronization to protect access to the shared data in the callback functions. Let me add a mutex to protect the global connections array:

	BT_BAP_QOS_CFG_PREF(true, BT_GAP_LE_PHY_2M, 0x02, 10, 10000, 40000, 10000, 40000);

static K_MUTEX_DEFINE(connections_mutex);</new_str>

<str_replace_editor>/repo/tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>static void unicast_client_disable_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream,
				      enum bt_bap_ascs_rsp_code rsp_code,
				      enum bt_bap_ascs_reason reason)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);

	LOG_DBG("stream %p disable operation rsp_code %u reason %u", stream, rsp_code, reason);

	btp_send_ascs_operation_completed_ev(stream->conn, u_stream->ase_id, BT_ASCS_DISABLE_OP,
					     rsp_code == BT_BAP_ASCS_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS
						     ? BTP_ASCS_STATUS_SUCCESS
						     : BTP_ASCS_STATUS_FAILED);
}</old_str>
<new_str>static void unicast_client_disable_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream,
				      enum bt_bap_ascs_rsp_code rsp_code,
				      enum bt_bap_ascs_reason reason)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	uint8_t ase_id;

	LOG_DBG("stream %p disable operation rsp_code %u reason %u", stream, rsp_code, reason);

	k_mutex_lock(&connections_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	k_mutex_unlock(&connections_mutex);

	btp_send_ascs_operation_completed_ev(stream->conn, ase_id, BT_ASCS_DISABLE_OP,
					     rsp_code == BT_BAP_ASCS_RSP_CODE_SUCCESS
						     ? BTP_ASCS_STATUS_SUCCESS
						     : BTP_ASCS_STATUS_FAILED);
}</new_str>

<old_str>static void btp_send_ascs_cis_disconnected_ev(const struct bt_bap_stream *stream, uint8_t reason)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream;
	struct btp_ascs_cis_disconnected_ev ev;

	u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	__ASSERT(u_stream != NULL, "Failed to get unicast_stream from %p", stream);

	bt_addr_le_copy(&ev.address, bt_conn_get_dst(stream->conn));
	ev.ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	ev.cis_id = u_stream->cis_id;
	ev.reason = reason;

	tester_event(BTP_SERVICE_ID_ASCS, BTP_ASCS_EV_CIS_DISCONNECTED, &ev, sizeof(ev));
}</old_str>
<new_str>static void btp_send_ascs_cis_disconnected_ev(const struct bt_bap_stream *stream, uint8_t reason)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream;
	struct btp_ascs_cis_disconnected_ev ev;
	uint8_t ase_id, cis_id;

	u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	__ASSERT(u_stream != NULL, "Failed to get unicast_stream from %p", stream);

	k_mutex_lock(&connections_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	cis_id = u_stream->cis_id;
	k_mutex_unlock(&connections_mutex);

	bt_addr_le_copy(&ev.address, bt_conn_get_dst(stream->conn));
	ev.ase_id = ase_id;
	ev.cis_id = cis_id;
	ev.reason = reason;

	tester_event(BTP_SERVICE_ID_ASCS, BTP_ASCS_EV_CIS_DISCONNECTED, &ev, sizeof(ev));
}</new_str>

Now let me fix the `stream_configured_cb` function:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c</str_replace_editor> [530, 545]

}

static void stream_configured_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream,
				 const struct bt_bap_qos_cfg_pref *pref)
{
	struct bt_bap_ep_info info;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_connection *u_conn;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	uint8_t ase_id;

	LOG_DBG("stream %p", stream);

	err = bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &info);
	if (err != 0) {
		LOG_ERR("Failed to get ep info: %d", err);

		return;
	}

	u_stream->conn_id = bt_conn_index(stream->conn);
	u_conn = &connections[u_stream->conn_id];

	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;

	if (info.dir == BT_AUDIO_DIR_SOURCE) {
		btp_send_ascs_operation_completed_ev(stream->conn, ase_id,
						     BT_ASCS_CONFIG_OP, BTP_ASCS_STATUS_SUCCESS);
	}
}</old_str>
<new_str>	btp_send_ascs_ase_state_changed_ev(stream->conn, u_stream->ase_id, info.state);
}

static void stream_configured_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream,
				 const struct bt_bap_qos_cfg_pref *pref)
{
	struct bt_bap_ep_info info;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_connection *u_conn;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	uint8_t ase_id;

	LOG_DBG("stream %p", stream);

	err = bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &info);
	if (err != 0) {
		LOG_ERR("Failed to get ep info: %d", err);

		return;
	}

	k_mutex_lock(&connections_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	u_stream->conn_id = bt_conn_index(stream->conn);
	u_conn = &connections[u_stream->conn_id];
	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	k_mutex_unlock(&connections_mutex);

	if (info.dir == BT_AUDIO_DIR_SOURCE) {
		btp_send_ascs_operation_completed_ev(stream->conn, ase_id,
						     BT_ASCS_CONFIG_OP, BTP_ASCS_STATUS_SUCCESS);
	}
}</new_str>

				     enum bt_bap_ascs_rsp_code rsp_code,

Let me check the unicast_client_start_cb function:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c</str_replace_editor> [925, 945]
<str_replace_editor>/repo/tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c</str_replace_editor> [945, 965]

<old_str>static void unicast_client_start_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream,
				    enum bt_bap_ascs_rsp_code rsp_code,
				    enum bt_bap_ascs_reason reason)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	struct bt_bap_ep_info ep_info;
	int err;

	/* Callback called on Receiver Start Ready notification from ASE Control Point */

	LOG_DBG("stream %p start operation rsp_code %u reason %u", stream, rsp_code, reason);
	u_stream->already_sent = false;

	err = bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &ep_info);
	if (err != 0) {
		LOG_ERR("Failed to get ep info: %d", err);

		return;
	}

	if (ep_info.dir == BT_AUDIO_DIR_SOURCE) {
		btp_send_ascs_operation_completed_ev(stream->conn, u_stream->ase_id,
						     BT_ASCS_START_OP, BTP_ASCS_STATUS_SUCCESS);
	}
}</old_str>
<new_str>static void unicast_client_start_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream,
				    enum bt_bap_ascs_rsp_code rsp_code,
				    enum bt_bap_ascs_reason reason)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	struct bt_bap_ep_info ep_info;
	int err;
	uint8_t ase_id;

	/* Callback called on Receiver Start Ready notification from ASE Control Point */

	LOG_DBG("stream %p start operation rsp_code %u reason %u", stream, rsp_code, reason);
	
	k_mutex_lock(&connections_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	u_stream->already_sent = false;
	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	k_mutex_unlock(&connections_mutex);

	err = bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &ep_info);
	if (err != 0) {
		LOG_ERR("Failed to get ep info: %d", err);

		return;
	}

	if (ep_info.dir == BT_AUDIO_DIR_SOURCE) {
		btp_send_ascs_operation_completed_ev(stream->conn, ase_id,
						     BT_ASCS_START_OP, BTP_ASCS_STATUS_SUCCESS);
	}
}</new_str>

<old_str>static void stream_state_changed(struct bt_bap_stream *stream)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	struct bt_bap_ep_info info;
	int err;

	if (stream->ep == NULL) {
		info.state = BT_BAP_EP_STATE_IDLE;
	} else {
		err = bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &info);
		if (err != 0) {
			LOG_ERR("Failed to get info: %d", err);

			return;
		}
	}

	btp_send_ascs_ase_state_changed_ev(stream->conn, u_stream->ase_id, info.state);
}</old_str>
<new_str>static void stream_state_changed(struct bt_bap_stream *stream)
{
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	struct bt_bap_ep_info info;
	int err;
	uint8_t ase_id;

	if (stream->ep == NULL) {
		info.state = BT_BAP_EP_STATE_IDLE;
	} else {
		err = bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &info);
		if (err != 0) {
			LOG_ERR("Failed to get info: %d", err);

			return;
		}
	}

	k_mutex_lock(&connections_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	k_mutex_unlock(&connections_mutex);

	btp_send_ascs_ase_state_changed_ev(stream->conn, ase_id, info.state);
}</new_str>

<str_replace_editor>/repo/tests/bluetooth/tester/src/audio/btp_bap_unicast.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>static void stream_released_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream)
{
	uint8_t cig_id;
	struct bt_bap_ep_info info;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_connection *u_conn;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	struct bt_conn *conn;

	LOG_DBG("Released stream %p", stream);

	u_conn = &connections[u_stream->conn_id];

	/* TODO: Fix this as stream->ep is always NULL in the released callback */
	if (stream->ep != NULL) {
		(void)bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &info);
		if (info.dir == BT_AUDIO_DIR_SINK) {
			u_conn->configured_sink_stream_count--;
		} else {
			u_conn->configured_source_stream_count--;
		}
	}

	conn = bt_conn_lookup_addr_le(BT_ID_DEFAULT, &u_conn->address);
	if (conn == NULL) {
		LOG_ERR("Unknown connection");
		return;
	}

	btp_send_ascs_ase_state_changed_ev(conn, u_stream->ase_id, BT_BAP_EP_STATE_IDLE);
	bt_conn_unref(conn);

	cig_id = u_stream->cig_id;
	btp_bap_unicast_stream_free(u_stream);

	if (cigs[cig_id].in_use == true &&
	    u_conn->configured_sink_stream_count == 0 &&
	    u_conn->configured_source_stream_count == 0) {
		struct btp_bap_unicast_group *u_cig = &cigs[cig_id];

		/* The unicast group will be deleted only at release of the last stream */
		LOG_DBG("Deleting unicast group");
		bt_bap_unicast_group_delete(u_cig->cig);
		u_cig->in_use = false;
	}
}</old_str>
<new_str>static void stream_released_cb(struct bt_bap_stream *stream)
{
	uint8_t cig_id;
	struct bt_bap_ep_info info;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_connection *u_conn;
	struct btp_bap_unicast_stream *u_stream = stream_bap_to_unicast(stream);
	struct bt_conn *conn;
	uint8_t ase_id;

	LOG_DBG("Released stream %p", stream);

	k_mutex_lock(&connections_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	u_conn = &connections[u_stream->conn_id];
	ase_id = u_stream->ase_id;
	cig_id = u_stream->cig_id;
	k_mutex_unlock(&connections_mutex);

	/* TODO: Fix this as stream->ep is always NULL in the released callback */
	if (stream->ep != NULL) {
		(void)bt_bap_ep_get_info(stream->ep, &info);
		if (info.dir == BT_AUDIO_DIR_SINK) {
			u_conn->configured_sink_stream_count--;
		} else {
			u_conn->configured_source_stream_count--;
		}
	}

	conn = bt_conn_lookup_addr_le(BT_ID_DEFAULT, &u_conn->address);
	if (conn == NULL) {
		LOG_ERR("Unknown connection");
		return;
	}

	btp_send_ascs_ase_state_changed_ev(conn, ase_id, BT_BAP_EP_STATE_IDLE);
	bt_conn_unref(conn);

	btp_bap_unicast_stream_free(u_stream);

	if (cigs[cig_id].in_use == true &&
	    u_conn->configured_sink_stream_count == 0 &&
	    u_conn->configured_source_stream_count == 0) {
		struct btp_bap_unicast_group *u_cig = &cigs[cig_id];

		/* The unicast group will be deleted only at release of the last stream */
		LOG_DBG("Deleting unicast group");
		bt_bap_unicast_group_delete(u_cig->cig);
		u_cig->in_use = false;
	}
}</new_str>
